sections.paragraph: & מַחֲרֶשֶׁת f. 1Sa 13:20, two instruments merits of husbandry, both with edges, one of which perhaps denoted the ploughshare (from the root חָרַשׁ No. 3), the other the coulter. For the pl. of both מַחֲרֵשֹׁות is used verse 21 1Sa 13:21. As to the form of ploughs in the East, see Paulsen, Ackerbau d. Morgenländer, page 52; Niebuhr’s Description of Arabia, page 155; as to the Egyptian plough, Description de l’Egypte, i. tab. 70, 71.
